Toggle navigation Diplocarpon fragariae (DIPCEA) Menu Overview → Categorization Documents Overview Code created in: 2001-10-12 Basic information EPPO Code: DIPCEA Preferred name: Diplocarpon fragariae Authority: (Libert) Rossman Other scientific names Name Authority Diplocarpon earlianum (Ellis & Everhart) F.A.Wolf Fabraea earliana (Ellis & Everhart) Seaver Gloeosporium fragariae (Libert) Montagne Leptothyrium fragariae Libert Marssoniella fragariae (Libert) von Höhnel Marssonina fragariae (Libert) Klebahn Peziza earliana Ellis & Everhart Common names Name Language leaf scorch of strawberry English leaf spot of strawberry English Rotfleckenkrankheit: Erdbeere German scorch des feuilles du fraisier French taches pourpres du fraisier French viruela de las hojas de la fresa Spanish Propose photo Taxonomy Kingdom Fungi      (
      1FUNGK      ) Phylum Ascomycota      (
      1ASCOP      ) Subphylum Pezizomycotina      (
      1PEZIQ      ) Class Leotiomycetes      (
      1LETOC      ) Order Helotiales      (
      1HELOO      ) Family Drepanopezizaceae      (
      1DRPPF      ) Genus Diplocarpon      (
      1DIPCG      ) Species Diplocarpon fragariae      (
      DIPCEA      )

seed.symptoms: Irregular dark purple spots on leaves; central tissue dies giving scorched appearance; severe infection leads to defoliation.
seed.causes: Fungus Diplocarpon earlianum; spreads via rain splash; favored by warm, wet conditions.
seed.treatment: Captan or myclobutanil fungicides; remove infected leaves.
seed.prevention: Plant in well-drained sites; avoid overhead irrigation; use disease-free transplants; mulch to reduce splash.
